skill.name: canvas-design
skill.description: Guides AI tools through creating a visual design philosophy and expressing it as polished PNG or PDF artwork.
skill.summary: Create design-forward static artwork with a written visual philosophy and polished canvas output.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Most static findings are false positives from bundled font license text, Apache license text, and design prose. The Ruby or shell backtick finding is markdown formatting around a directory path, not command execution. A medium-risk issue remains because the skill tells the model to download any needed fonts without trusted-source or approval guardrails.

medium_findings.title: Unbounded External Font Download Instruction
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the model to download whatever fonts are needed. This can trigger arbitrary external network access and introduce untrusted font files unless sources and user approval are constrained.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction explicitly permits downloads, but no destination or exfiltration endpoint is specified. The risk is uncontrolled network access and supply-chain exposure, not confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Bundled License URL Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to Apache and SIL Open Font License references. These are documentation links in license text, not executable network calls or data transfer logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are license notices and source references. No code path consumes these URLs or sends data to them.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance matches occur in ordinary design wording, Apache license clauses, and SIL license terms. No cryptographic API, hashing operation, host inspection, or reconnaissance behavior was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is natural language or standard license language. The skill contains instructions and font assets, not executable crypto or system probing code.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Finding Is A False Positive
low_findings.description: The external command finding is caused by markdown backticks around a local directory name. It does not invoke a shell or interpolate user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 108 uses inline code formatting for ./canvas-fonts. There is no command syntax, shell execution directive, or dynamic argument construction.

dangerous_patterns.title: Uncontrolled Network Retrieval Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes guidance to retrieve fonts from outside the bundled assets. This should be constrained to trusted sources and explicit user consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is encouraged by instruction rather than implemented code. Risk depends on the host tool policy and user approval flow.

content.value_statement: Static artwork often lacks a clear creative system. This skill turns a request into a design philosophy, then guides polished PNG or PDF output.
actual_capabilities: Creates a written visual philosophy before generating artwork.
Guides single-page PNG or PDF visual compositions.
Uses bundled font files for varied typography choices.
Emphasizes minimal text, visual hierarchy, spacing, and composition.
Supports multi-page visual series when the user requests it.
Adds a refinement pass focused on polish and cohesion.
limitations: It is a prompt skill, not a standalone rendering engine.
Output quality depends on the host tool and available file creation features.
It is not designed for text-heavy reports or editable layout systems.
External font downloads should require trusted sources and user approval.

use_cases.title: Event Poster Concept
use_cases.description: Create a high-impact poster with minimal copy, strong composition, and a defined visual movement.

use_cases.title: Editorial Art Direction
use_cases.description: Turn a theme or reference into an abstract visual system for magazine-style artwork.

use_cases.title: Presentation Cover Artwork
use_cases.description: Generate a polished cover image or PDF page that feels crafted and conceptually consistent.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Poster
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one finished artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a single-page PNG poster about urban renewal. Use minimal text and build a clear design philosophy first.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Mood Artwork
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a visual direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a PDF artwork for a quiet ceramics studio. Make it refined, tactile, and mostly visual.

prompt_templates.title: Reference-Driven Composition
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with a subtle concept.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create abstract artwork that subtly references lunar cartography. Keep the reference discoverable but not literal.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Page Visual Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a three-page PDF visual series for a climate research symposium. Each page should share one philosophy but feel distinct.

output_examples.input: Create a poster for a small jazz festival.
output_examples.output: A named design philosophy, followed by a polished poster file with restrained type, rhythmic forms, and subtle musical references.

output_examples.input: Make an abstract cover for a research memo about memory.
output_examples.output: A concise philosophy document and a single-page PDF cover with layered marks, careful spacing, and minimal anchor text.

output_examples.input: Build a visual series for a product launch.
output_examples.output: A connected set of pages that share typography, palette, and composition rules while varying the visual treatment.

best_practices: Provide the subject, desired format, dimensions, and any text that must appear.
Use bundled fonts first, and approve any external font source before download.
Ask for one clear artifact type when speed and polish matter most.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for long-form documents that need dense readable text.
Do not ask it to copy a living artist or protected brand style.
Do not allow arbitrary font downloads without reviewing the source.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a design philosophy document and guides PNG or PDF artwork based on that philosophy.

faq.question: Can it make multi-page outputs?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports multi-page PDF or PNG series when the user asks for more than one page.

faq.question: Does it include fonts?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill bundles many font files and instructs the model to search the canvas-fonts directory.

faq.question: Is it safe to use external fonts?
faq.answer: External fonts should only come from trusted sources and should require user approval before download.

faq.question: Is this useful for text-heavy documents?
faq.answer: No. It is best for highly visual static artwork with sparse, intentional text.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
